unsupported_implied.text: The product must integrate with the corporate directory or support SSO/directory-sync so that every login ties back to the company directory or a pre‑approved guest ID.
unsupported_implied.source_snippet: "Every login must tie back to our directory or a pre-approved guest ID."
unsupported_implied.reason: The source explicitly requires logins to be tied to the corporate directory or pre‑approved guest IDs and mentions directory sync/SSO as a desired mechanism. The gold set requires that only employees or approved external users may log in but does not require directory integration or SSO/directory‑sync as the authentication mechanism.

unsupported_implied.text: Exception log transmission must include secure transmission and built‑in PII redaction/anonymization before logs are sent to vendor support.
unsupported_implied.source_snippet: "Exception logging: capture exceptions with enough context for diagnosis; provide a way to transmit logs securely to vendor support. Privacy approach [TBD]."
unsupported_implied.reason: While the gold set requires creation/transmission of exception logs, the source adds a requirement for secure transmission and explicit redaction/anonymization of PII in logs. The gold requirements do not specify secure transport or PII redaction guidelines for transmitted logs.

unsupported_implied.text: Notifications to building/facilities personnel must include delivery tracking (confirming that the alert was sent to the designated distribution lists).
unsupported_implied.source_snippet: "Send it to our designated lists and track that the alert went out."
unsupported_implied.reason: The gold set requires notifying building personnel of equipment transport requests but does not require tracking or confirming that those notification messages were actually delivered to designated lists. The source explicitly demands delivery tracking as part of the notification behavior.
